what_it_does: Node CLI proof script that reads a DNA grammar JSON file, derives spacing, radius, type, and layout zone values from grammar rules, compares them against measured examples with tolerances, prints pass/fail proof output, and logs a worked trizone geometry derivation.
observations: Reads grammar data from ../dna/grammar.json relative to the engine directory.
Defines resolver helpers for units, spacing, radius, display type, and proportional zones.
Uses Math.round for all derived pixel-like values except the raw unit helper.
Compares derived values to measured values through a shared check helper with tolerance.
Prints grouped proof sections for scales, proportion, multi-scale behavior, density dials, and a worked example.
The worked example derives margin, gap, content width, plate width, corner radius, and title size from grammar inputs.
standouts: The file is both executable proof harness and small resolver implementation.
The derivations are intentionally presented as evidence that values come from rules instead of typed pixel constants.
The zone resolver treats archetype fractions as scale-free proportions applied to an axis length.
conventions: Uses ES modules and Node built-in imports.
Keeps resolver functions file-local and side-effect free until the proof output section.
Uses compact arrow helpers for simple predicates and format markers.
Uses console output as the proof/reporting surface.
Uses fallback multipliers of 1 for unknown tiers and densities.
concerns: The run comment references node dna/resolve.mjs, which does not match the provided file path.
Unknown tier or density names silently fall back to 1, which may hide misspellings in proof inputs.
zones assumes the archetype exists and will throw if the name is missing from the grammar.
The proof data includes measured constants inline, so the script demonstrates derivation but still embeds its comparison targets locally.
notes: This file is a local proof harness showing that grammar rules can reproduce selected measured layout and scale values.
questions: Should unknown tiers and densities be rejected instead of defaulting to multiplier 1?
Should missing zone archetypes produce a formatted proof failure rather than an exception?
Is the run-path comment intentionally different from this file path?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it derives values from grammar rules and checks them against measured layout and scale values, then prints a worked geometry derivation.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make the design grammar auditable by showing concrete measured outputs re-created from a small set of rules and dials.
novelty: It frames a design-token resolver as a proof script, using console checks to argue that layout values are consequences of grammar rather than independent constants.
contribution: It contributes a compact resolver-plus-proof layer that turns grammar JSON into derived measurements, validates those measurements against examples, and documents a worked geometry derivation that a fused system would preserve as explanatory infrastructure.
summary_for_embedding: Executable Node proof script that derives design scale, spacing, radius, typography, and proportional layout values from a grammar JSON file and compares them to measured examples.
intention_for_embedding: This was for demonstrating and auditing that a design grammar can reproduce locked visual geometry through rules, ratios, tiers, and dials.

symbols.does: Returns the base unit scaled by the selected tier multiplier.
symbols.description: This helper reads G.base.unit and multiplies it by G.scale_tiers.tiers[tier], defaulting the multiplier to 1 when the tier is missing. It is the base numeric primitive used by the spacing and radius derivations.

symbols.does: Derives a rounded spacing value for a named spacing step, tier, and density.
symbols.description: This function looks up a spacing step by name, applies the selected tier unit and density multiplier, then rounds the result. It encodes the file's idea that spacing values are produced from unit, step, and density rather than stored directly.

symbols.does: Derives a rounded radius value from the tier unit and signature ratio exponent.
symbols.description: This function computes unit(tier) multiplied by the signature ratio raised to n. It is used to reproduce the radius scale at slide and component tiers.

symbols.does: Derives a rounded display type size from a pivot, tier multiplier, and signature ratio exponent.
symbols.description: This function uses the display type pivot from the grammar, scales it by the tier multiplier, and applies the signature ratio exponent. It parallels the radius resolver while using the type scale's own pivot value.

symbols.does: Resolves a proportional archetype into rounded pixel lengths along a supplied axis.
symbols.description: This function reads a proportion archetype from the grammar, uses its parts array when present, otherwise extracts numeric values from the archetype object, and multiplies each part by the concrete axis length. It turns scale-free layout fractions into measurable dimensions.

symbols.does: Returns whether two numbers are within a given absolute tolerance.
symbols.description: This predicate supports the proof checks by comparing derived and measured values with a tolerance window. It uses absolute difference only, with no relative tolerance.

symbols.does: Formats a boolean check result as an OK or XX marker.
symbols.description: This helper maps proof pass/fail state into the compact text prefix used in console output. The returned strings include trailing spaces for alignment.

symbols.does: Compares derived and measured values, updates pass totals, and prints a formatted proof line.
symbols.description: This function normalizes scalar or array inputs with [].concat, verifies length and tolerance for each value, increments counters, and logs the rule-derived values beside measured values. It is the central reporting mechanism for the proof sections.
